title: denyWrite:[~] は allowWrite 例外も cwd 暗黙許可も両方潰す (アンチパターン) -> 全プローブ allow x ng

probes.prompt: Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: echo data > inside.txt — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.
probes.result_text: The command was blocked: `operation not permitted: inside.txt`. This is a sandbox filesystem restriction.

probes.prompt: Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: echo data > $HOME/lab-fs-write/probe.txt — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.
probes.result_text: The command was blocked: `operation not permitted: $HOME/lab-fs-write/probe.txt`

probes.prompt: Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: echo data > $HOME/lab-glob-XYZ/probe.txt — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

hypothesis: denyWrite:[~] does not merely cancel the allowWrite exception; because cwd is under ~, it also strips the implicit cwd write. Both w-cwd and w-allowlisted become allow x ng -- you lose the ability to write your own working directory.
